title: Find emails & subdomains for a domain
description: Enter a domain and we harvest its emails, subdomains, IPs, URLs and ASNs from 54+ public sources. Everything except the domain is optional.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: The domain to investigate, for example itm.edu or github.com. Enter just the domain - we auto-clean full links, so https://www.itm.edu/ becomes itm.edu, and you can also enter an IP address. We then harvest its emails, subdomains, IPs, URLs and ASNs from public sources.

sources.title: Sources to search
sources.description: Which sources to search. The four defaults (crt.sh, HackerTarget, RapidDNS, CertSpotter) are free and need no API key. Sources marked with a key icon need an API key, which you add in the API keys section below. Sources marked with a tick are free.
sources.sectionDescription: Which sources to search and how many results to pull. The defaults are free and need no API key.

limit.title: Results per source
limit.description: Maximum number of results to pull from each source. Default 500. Lower it for faster, cheaper runs; raise it for deeper coverage.

start.title: Skip first N results
start.description: Skip the first N results from each source. Used for paging through very large result sets. Leave at 0 for normal runs.
start.sectionDescription: Extra recon features and tuning. The defaults are fine for most runs - you can leave this whole section alone.

timeout.title: Time limit (seconds)
timeout.description: Stop the scan after this many seconds and return whatever was found by then. Default 1800 (30 minutes). Raise it for very large domains.

dnsLookup.title: Resolve subdomains to IPs
dnsLookup.description: Resolve each discovered subdomain to its IP address. Slower, but adds the IP next to every subdomain.

dnsBrute.title: Find hidden subdomains
dnsBrute.description: Find hidden subdomains by trying a list of common names like mail, dev, vpn and staging. Slower, but uncovers subdomains that are not in public records.

dnsResolve.title: Custom resolver list (path)
dnsResolve.description: Path to your own list of DNS resolvers, for advanced users. Leave empty to use the built-in defaults.

dnsServer.title: Custom DNS server
dnsServer.description: Use a specific DNS server for lookups, for example 8.8.8.8. Leave empty to use the defaults.

shodan.title: Enrich hosts with Shodan
shodan.description: Enrich the discovered hosts with extra data from Shodan. Needs a Shodan API key in the API keys section below.

takeOver.title: Check for subdomain takeover
takeOver.description: Check the discovered subdomains for takeover risk - subdomains whose DNS still points at an unclaimed third-party service that someone else could claim.

screenshot.title: Screenshot each subdomain
screenshot.description: Take a screenshot of each discovered subdomain's home page. Screenshots are saved to the run's storage.

apiScan.title: Scan for exposed API endpoints
apiScan.description: Look for exposed or undocumented API endpoints on the domain. Works best together with a wordlist.

wordlist.title: Wordlist (path)
wordlist.description: Path to a wordlist used by the API endpoint scan. On Apify cloud, only wordlists already built into the image can be used.

useProxies.title: Route through proxies
useProxies.description: Route requests through proxies. Needs a proxy list configured in the image, so most users leave this off.

quiet.title: Hide missing-key warnings
quiet.description: Hide the missing API key warnings in the run log. On by default to keep the log clean.

shodanApiKey.title: Shodan API key
shodanApiKey.description: Your Shodan API key from shodan.io. Only needed if you select the Shodan source. Leave blank otherwise.
shodanApiKey.sectionCaption: API keys (optional - skip unless you have accounts)
shodanApiKey.sectionDescription: Most people leave every field here blank. The free default sources already return emails and subdomains. Add a key only for a source you have an account with. For the 30+ less common sources, use the last field (more API keys).

censysApiId.title: Censys API ID
censysApiId.description: Your Censys API ID from censys.io. Pairs with the Censys API secret below.

censysApiSecret.title: Censys API secret
censysApiSecret.description: Your Censys API secret from censys.io.

securitytrailsApiKey.title: SecurityTrails API key
securitytrailsApiKey.description: Your SecurityTrails API key from securitytrails.com. Adds strong subdomain coverage.

hunterApiKey.title: Hunter.io API key
hunterApiKey.description: Your Hunter.io API key from hunter.io. Adds email discovery.

virustotalApiKey.title: VirusTotal API key
virustotalApiKey.description: Your VirusTotal API key from virustotal.com.

githubToken.title: GitHub token
githubToken.description: Your GitHub personal access token. Needed to search public code on GitHub for mentions of the domain.

braveApiKey.title: Brave Search API key
braveApiKey.description: Your Brave Search API key from brave.com/search/api. A free tier is available.

extraApiKeys.title: More API keys (advanced)
extraApiKeys.description: Keys for the other 30+ premium sources, as one JSON object. Use each provider's field name as the key, for example: {"netlasApiKey": "...", "fofaKey": "...", "fofaEmail": "you@example.com", "intelxApiKey": "..."}. Only needed for premium sources you selected that are not listed above. Supported names: hunterhowApiKey, intelxApiKey, onypheApiKey, fullhuntApiKey, netlasApiKey, leakixApiKey, leaklookupApiKey, zoomeyeApiKey, bevigilApiKey, bitbucketApiKey, bufferoverunApiKey, builtwithApiKey, chaosApiKey, criminalipApiKey, dehashedApiKey, dnsdumpsterApiKey, dymoApiKey, fofaKey, fofaEmail, hackertargetApiKey, hibpApiKey, mojeekApiKey, pentesttoolsApiKey, projectdiscoveryApiKey, rocketreachApiKey, securityscorecardApiKey, subdomainfinderc99ApiKey, tombaKey, tombaSecret, venacusApiKey, whoisxmlApiKey, windvaneApiKey.
